explanations.9: This line shows that the validator found a JAR file (a compiled Java library) in the local Maven cache, and it extracted a configuration file called kmodule.xml from inside it.
explanations.10: The system is now preparing to use the validation rules from that JAR file by creating a KieModule, which is a container that holds business rules.
explanations.11: The validator noticed this JAR file has an 'executable model', meaning the rules were pre-compiled for faster execution instead of being interpreted on-the-fly.
explanations.12: The system is building 'defaultKieBase', which is essentially the knowledge base that will be used to check your API against all the validation rules.
explanations.27: This is a conflict: OpenAPI 3.1 is newer but not widely adopted yet, so the validator recommends sticking with OpenAPI 3.0 for better compatibility with other tools.
